mechanism_narrative: A rising federal funds rate raises the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows and tightens financial conditions, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days when the rate trend is upward.
failure_condition: Rank IC between FEDFUNDS trend and 20d forward returns stays below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or returns during rate-hike periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rise in the unemployment rate of more than 0.2 percentage points over a rolling 3-month window signals labor market deterioration that precedes earnings downgrades, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: In more than 55% of trigger instances, 20-day forward returns are positive, or the signal shows no statistically significant negative mean return relative to non-triggered periods (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Joint acceleration in retail sales and housing starts indicates broad-based consumer and investment demand strength that supports corporate revenue growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days when both series rise simultaneously; the effect is not present when only one series accelerates.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS 3-month annualized growth > 3% AND HOUST 3-month change > 0
failure_condition: Joint-trigger 60-day forward returns are not significantly higher than the unconditional baseline (p > 0.05), or single-series triggers produce equally strong returns, indicating no interaction effect.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month growth in industrial production reflects expanding real output capacity and corporate profitability, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days as investors price in continued supply-side momentum.
failure_condition: Rank IC between INDPRO monthly growth and 5-day forward returns is below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or the sign of the correlation flips persistently for more than 6 consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate rises faster than CPI inflation, real interest rates increase, tightening effective financial conditions beyond nominal appearances and compressing equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days; neither series alone captures this effect.
failure_condition: The interaction term (FEDFUNDS change minus CPIAUCSL inflation change) shows no significant incremental predictive power over either series individually (partial R^2 < 0.01) across rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A payroll employment print that surprises meaningfully above trailing 6-month average growth signals stronger-than-expected economic momentum, causing a short-term positive S&P 500 reaction over the next 5 trading days as risk appetite increases.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS monthly change exceeds trailing 6-month average by more than 50,000 jobs
failure_condition: Triggered instances show mean 5-day forward return not significantly different from unconditional baseline (p > 0.05), or negative returns occur in more than 45% of triggers.

mechanism_narrative: In a late-cycle expansion where GDP growth is decelerating from prior quarters while remaining positive, equity markets price in a higher probability of recession within the next quarter, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days; in early or mid-cycle regimes, decelerating growth does not carry the same negative signal.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns following GDP deceleration in late-cycle regimes are not significantly more negative than in early/mid-cycle deceleration episodes (difference-in-means p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production growth crosses from positive to negative on a 3-month annualized basis, it signals an emerging manufacturing contraction that historically precedes broader earnings weakness, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns after the crossing event are positive in more than 55% of instances, or the mean return is not significantly below the unconditional baseline (p > 0.05).
